_doc.hook_md_contract: T1171: Each hook script ('.ps1') has a companion '.md' self-description file at the same path (e.g. '.claude/hooks/g-hk-session-start.md' for '.claude/hooks/g-hk-session-start.ps1'). The hook.md uses the 5-section template (# Hook / ## Fires On / ## What It Does / ## Side Effects / ## Related Tasks) defined by '/g-hook-create'. The runtime contract is: when a hook fires under PreToolUse (or any other event), the harness SHOULD read the matching hook.md and inject its content as additional_context so the agent knows what the hook just did or why a tool call was blocked. See 'skills/g-skl-platform-claude/SKILL.md' Section 3 and 'skills/g-skl-platform-cursor/SKILL.md' Section 11.
_doc.hook_md_authoring: Use '/g-hook-create <hook-name> <event>' to scaffold a new pair atomically. See 'commands/g-hook-create.md'.
_doc.hook_md_source_of_truth: Source the human-facing hook description from the 'hook.md' file; this 'hooks.json' is for wiring only.
_doc.event_names: Claude Code official PascalCase event names: SessionStart, SessionEnd, Stop, SubagentStop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Notification, PreCompact. 'beforeShellExecution' is a Cursor-era name with no Claude Code equivalent — use PreToolUse with a Bash|Shell matcher instead.
_doc.gald3r_lifecycle_events: T1055: In addition to the harness-native events wired above, gald3r defines four gald3r-internal lifecycle events: 'pre_skill', 'post_skill', 'pre_session', 'post_session'. Cursor and Claude Code do NOT expose native skill-boundary or gald3r-session-boundary events, so these are dispatched by the gald3r skill/command runner (or fired manually) and are NOT auto-wired here.
_doc.hooks_json_is_supported: Claude Code reads this top-level .claude/hooks.json file directly (confirmed current method). The settings.json 'hooks' key is an alternate surface; hooks.json is preferred by gald3r for explicit wiring with _hook_md companion references.
